articles.headline: Painesville Riverside High School student arrested after being found with gun in school, officials say
articles.image_alt: Riverside High School in Painesville, Ohio
articles.excerpt: A Riverside High School student was arrested April 24 after administrators received a tip about a threatening image posted to social media from inside the school. Lake County deputies found the student in a classroom with a firearm. The lockdown was lifted within 22 minutes; no one was injured.

articles.headline: Swansea parents arraigned after second-grader allegedly brought gun to school
articles.excerpt: Christopher and Heather Spangler of Swansea were each arraigned Wednesday on 13 firearm and child-endangerment charges after police say their second-grader brought a handgun to Hoyle Elementary School in his backpack. Both pleaded not guilty and were ordered to surrender all firearms.

articles.headline: Three MLK High School students charged with battery after fighting teacher
articles.image_alt: Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. High School entrance sign
articles.excerpt: Three students at Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. High School in DeKalb County, Georgia were charged with battery and disrupting public school after a hallway fight with a veteran teacher on April 22. Video of the incident circulated widely on social media and is being used by district investigators.

articles.headline: AI phone call prompts lockdown at South Sioux City High School, police say
articles.image_alt: South Sioux City High School exterior
articles.excerpt: An AI-generated phone call claiming a pipe bomb in the building and a shotgun on campus triggered a lockdown at South Sioux City High School on April 22. Police, sheriff's deputies, and Nebraska State Patrol cleared the school within an hour. No threat was found.

articles.headline: Student arrested at Righetti High School with loaded handgun, second such arrest this school year
articles.image_alt: Righetti High School campus in Santa Maria
articles.excerpt: An 18-year-old student was arrested at Righetti High School in Santa Maria on April 22 after a school resource deputy serving an unrelated warrant discovered a loaded handgun in his possession. He was booked on felony possession of a firearm on school grounds.

articles.headline: Grissom High School student arrested after weapons-detection system flags pistol at entrance
articles.image_alt: Grissom High School in Huntsville, Alabama
articles.excerpt: A 16-year-old Grissom High School junior was arrested April 21 after Huntsville's campus weapons-detection system flagged a handgun in his backpack as he tried to enter. The student never made it inside and has been charged with unlawful possession of a pistol by a minor.

articles.headline: Dozens of first responders flood Valley Forge High School after cafeteria shooting
articles.image_alt: Emergency responders at Valley Forge High School
articles.excerpt: A student died after a shooting in the cafeteria of Valley Forge High School in Parma Heights, Ohio on April 20. The incident prompted an immediate lockdown and response from dozens of first responders from across the region.

articles.headline: Oklahoma principal shot in leg is praised for tackling school shooter: 'He is a hero'
articles.image_alt: Pauls Valley High School Principal Kirk Moore
articles.excerpt: Principal Kirk Moore tackled a 20-year-old gunman at Pauls Valley High School on April 7, disarming him with the help of an assistant principal. Moore was shot in the leg during the struggle but held the suspect until police arrived.

articles.headline: Student violence causes SC teachers to quit. Legislators are working on a solution.
articles.excerpt: South Carolina educators are leaving the profession over escalating student violence, with reports of concussions, torn tendons, and one miscarriage caused by student assaults. A House bill to remove repeatedly violent students stalled in committee over equity concerns.

articles.headline: Bill advances to protect teachers, but critics warn of impact on students of color and rural parishes
articles.excerpt: Louisiana House Bill 283 — the Teacher Shield Act — cleared the House on April 8 and is now under Senate review. The bill would require immediate removal and alternative placement for students who assault teachers, plus expanded leave for educator recovery.

archive.headline: Teacher charged after hoax triggers lockdown at Splendora High School, sheriff says
archive.excerpt: A Splendora, Texas high school teacher was charged with filing a false report and tampering with evidence after investigators determined that an alleged student assault that triggered a campus lockdown on April 9 was a hoax with self-inflicted injuries.

archive.headline: Capitol Access Minute: Legislation advances, along with Teacher Shield Act
archive.excerpt: Louisiana Public Radio's Capitol Access Minute covers the April 8 advancement of the Teacher Shield Act (HB 283) through the House. Rep. Candace Newell, a former teacher, authored the bill; Sen. Gary Carter will carry it in the Senate.

archive.headline: Spanberger signs bipartisan school-safety, student support bills into Virginia law
archive.image_alt: Virginia Governor signing school safety legislation
archive.excerpt: Governor Abigail Spanberger signed a package of bipartisan bills on April 2, including HB592 to let districts equip teachers with wearable panic alarms, HB38 to train teachers on student mental health, and HB1071 strengthening red-flag training.

archive.headline: South Carolina lawmakers introduce 'Educator Safety and Classroom Authority Act of 2026'
archive.excerpt: House Bill 5483, the Educator Safety and Classroom Authority Act of 2026, was introduced April 1 and referred to the House Committee on Education and Public Works. The bill establishes educator authority in classroom discipline and protections from disruptive and violent student behavior.

archive.headline: Student dead after shooting teacher at Hill Country Preparatory High School in Comal ISD
archive.image_alt: Law enforcement at the scene of a school shooting
archive.excerpt: A 15-year-old student shot a teacher at Hill Country College Preparatory High School in Bulverde, Texas on March 30, then turned the gun on himself, according to the Comal County Sheriff's Office. The teacher survived with injuries.
